name: Charter Act 1813
summary: Renewed the Company's charter but ended its trade monopoly over India (the China and tea trade monopoly was retained until 1833). For the first time appropriated Indian revenues for educational purposes and permitted Christian missionaries to operate in India.
key_provisions: Company's exclusive trade to India abolished; China and tea trade retained
British subjects may trade with India subject to Company licensing
One lakh rupees per annum appropriated for education and learning
Christian missionaries permitted to enter India subject to licence
Crown's sovereignty over British possessions in India explicitly declared
Company's territorial and political functions renewed for 20 years
